# Sequence Utilities


<!-- WARNING: THIS FILE WAS AUTOGENERATED! DO NOT EDIT! -->

## Introduction

This notebook contains utility functions for sequence manipulation,
barcode detection, and quality assessment in the BarcodeSeqKit library.
These utilities are format-agnostic and can be used with both BAM and
FASTQ data.

## Barcode Detection

Functions for detecting and validating barcodes in sequences.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/mtinti/BarcodeSeqKit/blob/main/BarcodeSeqKit/sequence_utils.py#L19"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### find_barcode_matches

>  find_barcode_matches (sequence:str,
>                            barcodes:List[BarcodeSeqKit.core.BarcodeConfig],
>                            max_mismatches:int=0)

\*Find all barcode matches in a sequence.

Args: sequence: The DNA/RNA sequence to search in barcodes: List of
barcode configurations to search for max_mismatches: Maximum number of
mismatches to allow

Returns: List of BarcodeMatch objects representing the matches found\*

## Sequence Manipulation

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/mtinti/BarcodeSeqKit/blob/main/BarcodeSeqKit/sequence_utils.py#L89"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### reverse_complement

>  reverse_complement (sequence:str)

\*Return the reverse complement of a DNA sequence.

Args: sequence: DNA sequence

Returns: Reverse complement of the sequence\*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/mtinti/BarcodeSeqKit/blob/main/BarcodeSeqKit/sequence_utils.py#L101"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### hamming_distance

>  hamming_distance (seq1:str, seq2:str)

\*Calculate the Hamming distance between two sequences.

The sequences must be of the same length.

Args: seq1: First sequence seq2: Second sequence

Returns: Hamming distance (number of positions where the sequences
differ)

Raises: ValueError: If sequences have different lengths\*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/mtinti/BarcodeSeqKit/blob/main/BarcodeSeqKit/sequence_utils.py#L122"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### find_best_barcode_match

>  find_best_barcode_match (sequence:str,
>                               barcodes:List[BarcodeSeqKit.core.BarcodeConfig],
>                               max_mismatches:int=1)

\*Find the best matching barcode in a sequence.

Args: sequence: The sequence to search in barcodes: List of barcode
configurations to search for max_mismatches: Maximum number of
mismatches to allow

Returns: The best matching BarcodeMatch or None if no match found\*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/mtinti/BarcodeSeqKit/blob/main/BarcodeSeqKit/sequence_utils.py#L179"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### classify_read_by_first_match

>  classify_read_by_first_match (sequence:str,
>                                    barcodes:List[BarcodeSeqKit.core.BarcodeCon
>                                    fig], max_mismatches:int=0)

\*Classify a read based on the first barcode match found.

This is an optimized version that stops after finding the first match,
without evaluating all possible matches in the sequence.

Args: sequence: The sequence to classify barcodes: List of barcode
configurations to search for max_mismatches: Maximum number of
mismatches to allow

Returns: Tuple of (match, category) Category is one of:
“barcode5_orientFR”, “barcode5_orientRC”, etc. or “noBarcode” if no
match found\*

# Barcode Classification Utilities

Functions for classifying and categorizing barcode matches.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

<a
href="https://github.com/mtinti/BarcodeSeqKit/blob/main/BarcodeSeqKit/sequence_utils.py#L283"
target="_blank" style="float:right; font-size:smaller">source</a>

### get_output_category

>  get_output_category (match:Optional[BarcodeSeqKit.core.BarcodeMatch],
>                           single_barcode_mode:bool=False)

\*Get the output category for a barcode match.

Args: match: Barcode match or None single_barcode_mode: Whether we’re in
single barcode mode

Returns: Category string for output file naming\*

``` python
# Example sequence with a barcode
sequence = "AAAAAATCGCGAGGCAAAAAAAGGCCGGCCGGAAAAAA"

# Create barcode configurations

barcode_5prime = BarcodeConfig(
    sequence="TCGCGAGGC",
    location=BarcodeLocationType.FIVE_PRIME,
    name="5",
    description="5' barcode for phenotyping experiment"
)

barcode_3prime = BarcodeConfig(
    sequence="GGCCGGCCGG",
    location=BarcodeLocationType.THREE_PRIME,
    name="3",
    description="3' barcode for phenotyping experiment"
)


# Find barcode matches
matches = find_barcode_matches(sequence, [barcode_5prime, barcode_3prime])
print(f"Found {len(matches)} matches")

for match in matches:
    print(f"Match: {match}")
    print(f"  Barcode: {match.barcode.name}")
    print(f"  Orientation: {match.orientation.value}")
    print(f"  Position: {match.position}")
    print(f"  Sequence: {match.sequence}")
```

    Found 2 matches
    Match: 5 (FR) at position 6
      Barcode: 5
      Orientation: FR
      Position: 6
      Sequence: TCGCGAGGC
    Match: 3 (FR) at position 22
      Barcode: 3
      Orientation: FR
      Position: 22
      Sequence: GGCCGGCCGG

``` python
sequence = "TTTTTTCCGGCCGGCCTTTTTTTGCCTCGCGATTTTTT"
# Create barcode configurations

barcode_5prime = BarcodeConfig(
    sequence="TCGCGAGGC",
    location=BarcodeLocationType.FIVE_PRIME,
    name="5",
    description="5' barcode for phenotyping experiment"
)

barcode_3prime = BarcodeConfig(
    sequence="GGCCGGCCGG",
    location=BarcodeLocationType.THREE_PRIME,
    name="3",
    description="3' barcode for phenotyping experiment"
)


# Find barcode matches
matches = find_barcode_matches(sequence, [barcode_5prime, barcode_3prime])
print(f"Found {len(matches)} matches")

for match in matches:
    print(f"Match: {match}")
    print(f"  Barcode: {match.barcode.name}")
    print(f"  Orientation: {match.orientation.value}")
    print(f"  Position: {match.position}")
    print(f"  Sequence: {match.sequence}")
```

    Found 2 matches
    Match: 5 (RC) at position 23
      Barcode: 5
      Orientation: RC
      Position: 23
      Sequence: GCCTCGCGA
    Match: 3 (RC) at position 6
      Barcode: 3
      Orientation: RC
      Position: 6
      Sequence: CCGGCCGGCC

``` python
sequence = "ACCCCCTTTCACCTTCAAACCGTTCCACTGTTCGTTAAGTGTTGTGATCATCGCCTTCGGCCGGCCTCAGTTAGCC"
barcode_5prime = BarcodeConfig(
    sequence="CTGACTCCTTAAGGGCC",
    location=BarcodeLocationType.FIVE_PRIME,
    name="5",
    description="5' barcode for phenotyping experiment"
)
barcode_3prime = BarcodeConfig(
    sequence="TAACTGAGGCCGGC",
    location=BarcodeLocationType.THREE_PRIME,
    name="3",
    description="3' barcode for phenotyping experiment"
)

# Find barcode matches
matches = find_barcode_matches(sequence, [barcode_5prime, barcode_3prime])
print(f"Found {len(matches)} matches")

for match in matches:
    print(f"Match: {match}")
    print(f"  Barcode: {match.barcode.name}")
    print(f"  Orientation: {match.orientation.value}")
    print(f"  Position: {match.position}")
    print(f"  Sequence: {match.sequence}")
    
    
# Classify a read
match, category = classify_read_by_first_match(
    sequence=sequence,
    barcodes=[barcode_5prime, barcode_3prime]
)
print(f"First match: {match}")
print(f"Category: {category}")
```

    Found 1 matches
    Match: 3 (RC) at position 59
      Barcode: 3
      Orientation: RC
      Position: 59
      Sequence: GCCGGCCTCAGTTA
    First match: 3 (RC) at position 59
    Category: barcode3_orientRC

``` python
# Example of classifying a read using the first match approach
sequence = "AAAAAATCGCGAGGCAAAAAAAGGCCGGCCGGAAAAAA"

# Classify a read
match, category = classify_read_by_first_match(
    sequence=sequence,
    barcodes=[barcode_5prime, barcode_3prime]
)
print(f"First match: {match}")
print(f"Category: {category}")
```

    First match: None
    Category: noBarcode

``` python
sequence = "TTTTTTCCGGCCGGCCTTTTTTTGCCTCGCGATTTTTT"
# Create barcode configurations
# Classify a read
match, category = classify_read_by_first_match(
    sequence=sequence,
    barcodes=[barcode_5prime, barcode_3prime]
)
print(f"First match: {match}")
print(f"Category: {category}")
```

    First match: None
    Category: noBarcode

``` python
# Example with fuzzy matching using regex
sequence_with_error = "ACGTCGCGAAGCTAGCTAGCTAGCTAGCTAGC"  # One mismatch
matches = find_barcode_matches(
    sequence=sequence_with_error,
    barcodes=[barcode_5prime, barcode_3prime],
    max_mismatches=1
)

print(f"Found {len(matches)} matches with max_mismatches=1")
for match in matches:
    print(f"Match: {match}")
    print(f"  Sequence: {match.sequence}")
    
    # Use regex to get fuzzy match details
    if match.orientation == OrientationType.FORWARD:
        pattern = f"({match.barcode.sequence}){{e<=1}}"
    else:
        pattern = f"({match.barcode.reverse_complement}){{e<=1}}"
        
    r = regex.compile(pattern)
    m = r.match(match.sequence)
    
    if m:
        subs, ins, dels = m.fuzzy_counts
        print(f"  Fuzzy counts: {subs} substitutions, {ins} insertions, {dels} deletions")
```

    Found 0 matches with max_mismatches=1

``` python
# Example of classifying a read using the first match approach
sequence = "ACGTCGCGAGGCTAGCTAGCTAGCTAGCTAGC"

# Classify a read
match, category = classify_read_by_first_match(
    sequence=sequence,
    barcodes=[barcode_5prime, barcode_3prime]
)

print(f"First match: {match}")
print(f"Category: {category}")

# Compare with a sequence that has multiple potential matches
sequence_with_multiple = "TCGCGAGGCTAGCTAGCGCCTCGCGATAGC"  # Contains both barcodes
match, category = classify_read_by_first_match(
    sequence=sequence_with_multiple,
    barcodes=[barcode_5prime, barcode_3prime]
)

print(f"\nWith multiple potential matches:")
print(f"First match: {match}")
print(f"Category: {category}")
```

    First match: None
    Category: noBarcode

    With multiple potential matches:
    First match: None
    Category: noBarcode

# Conclusion

This notebook provides utility functions for sequence manipulation,
barcode detection, and classification in BarcodeSeqKit. These functions
are used by both the BAM and FASTQ extractors to identify and categorize
barcoded reads.
